Additionally, 'return' (distractor) directly replaces 'returned' (same slot, base vs. inflected form, same position) — this is a 1:1 substitution producing 'i already return the books i borrowed last week' which is grammatical in informal/present contexts. Flag as blocker.|tmp_r1_q8: distractor DIRECTLY replaces one word — 'return' replaces 'returned' in the same position (slot 1 after prefill), producing a recognizable sentence with only a tense shift, no rearrangement required.

review:blocker:tmp_r2_q9: ungrammatical answer — 'tasted wonderfully rich' is non-standard; 'wonderfully' modifies adjectives but 'tasted wonderfully' reads as if 'wonderfully' is an adverb of manner on 'tasted'; the standard form is 'tasted wonderfully rich' which is marginal — more naturally 'tasted wonderfully' alone OR 'tasted rich and hearty'; borderline but likely to confuse test-takers and may be flagged by ETS editors
